mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are not significantly positive during low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0 percent indicates severe labor market slack and weak consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed zero in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE remains above 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 150 basis points over 60-day holding periods in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals supply chain bottlenecks and weak factory activity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of months where INDPRO declines during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero federal funds rates and declining unemployment confirms accommodative monetary policy supporting economic growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in a multivariate regression of 20-day forward returns over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail sales exceeding 1.0 percent signals immediate consumer demand contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of instances where retail sales drop by over 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal generates a negative cumulative return over 5-day horizons in more than 55 percent of expansionary months.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll employment growth during a late-cycle expansion triggers inflation and rate hike expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 200000 during late-cycle phases.
